repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Methodist Episcopal Church of Seneca Falls records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Methodist Episcopal Church of Seneca Falls (Seneca Falls, N.Y.)

did.abstract: Minutes and subscription lists pertaining to the history and operation of the church.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: In 1812 a small class existed at Seneca Falls on the Seneca Circuit. It joined with another class at Black Brook, and by 1830 the class numbered twenty-five of whom only one was a freeholder. The group had incorporated in 1829 as the Seneca Falls Methodist Episcopal Church and had begun to build a meeting house on donated land. When work on the church stalled because of lack of money, the minister recommended abandoning the project, but members suceeded in enclosing the roof and building seats. Revival meetings were held in the structure and seventy new members were added to the congregation. In 1834 land was donated for a parsonage. In 1843-44 a portion of the congregation seceeded and organized themselves as the Wesleyan Methodist Church (Collection #6049). In 1857 the church was remodeled. In 1865 a few members left to join the Free Methodist Movement. In 1871 the old church was torn down and a new building dedicated on July 24, 1872 was constructed. In 1876 the church had 230 members and 50 probationers and 250 scholars in the Sunday School.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Minutes begin in May 1856 and end in June 1871. Contains subscription lists (1851, 1852); and minutes of quarterly meetings of the Official Board (1856-1871), which included the minister, class leaders and stewards.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Methodist Episcopal Church of Seneca Falls records, #6007. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Seneca Falls (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
Seneca County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Methodist church buildings.
Church records and registers -- New York (State) -- Seneca Falls.
Methodist Episcopal Church -- New York (State) -- Seneca Falls.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes primarily concern the collection of subscriptions, the adjustment of pastor's salary, and the reports of the progress of collections. Some entries concern disciplinary cases having to do with disputes within the congregation. Items of note include:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Individuals withdraw from church for various reasons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arrangements made for a district campmeeting and a mission Sunday School in the Southwest part of the village.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Decision to form anti-slavery meetings once a month.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concern over church members attending a fair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The subject of renting or taxing the pew slips is discussed. The congregation is polled.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preparations to repair and eventually rebuild the church.
